{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Compliance and Regulatory Coordinator",
  "description": "Nestoil is Nigeria&rsquo;s largest indigenous Engineering, Procurement, Construction and Commissioning (EPCC) Company in the Oil and Gas sector and has been a significant contributor to local content in the industry since inception about 30 years ago. With about 2,000 direct employees, Nestoil continues to redefine industry standards in Pipeline Construction, Repairs and Maintenance with associated facilities for Dredging, River Crossing and Shoreline Protection.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Compliance and Regulatory Coordinator

Job Location:&nbsp;Warri, Delta
SBU: White Dove Shipping Company Limited

Job Summary &amp; Purpose


	Accountable for managing all statutory/regulatory compliance framework applicable to White Dove Shipping Company activities such as international classification societies, flag states and all Nigerian authorities &ndash; NPA, NIMASA, DPR, NIWA, etc.
	These include all ISO compliance audits, local authorities&rsquo; inspections, ISM/vessel inspections and acquisitions of certificates, permits, licenses, consents, etc.


Responsibilities


	Validate the quality, safety and security systems of the Company and the vessel fleet at all times; ascertain its compliance with the industry and the Company standards.
	Pay regular visit to vessels for physical inspections to ensure all statutory compliance are in place.
	Conduct on board training, pre-vetting inspection and participate in the vetting inspections onboard the company managed vessels.
	Review and modify Procedures and Instructions where necessary to improve Safety Management System.
	Ensure all regulatory requirements in terms of documentations, certifications, authorization, procedures and regulations are complied with strictly.
	Process all DPR / Regulatory Barging permits.
	Plan, monitor and co-ordinate the vessels&rsquo; survey &amp; audit schedule.
	Maintain corrective action register for all Marine related audits and inspections as well following up on same.
	Monitor Flag State Control (NIMASA) on vessels Survey and Audit result.
	Manage and keep updated the list of publications required onboard the vessels and advise on a Company standard to follow.
	Implement and monitor Company ISM Procedures.
	Conduct Independent compliance reviews on Marine Terminal Management &amp; Self-Assessment (MTMSA) and Tanker Management &amp; Self-Assessment (TMSA) Audits, prepare and submit comprehensive report on findings;
	Follow up with vessels on review findings and effective close out of observations
	Represent the Company at OCIMF (Oil Company International Marine Forum) meetings.
	Manage the Company&rsquo;s SIRE (Ship Inspection Report Program) inspection process, conduct quality reviews of reports submitted and update OCIMF SIRE database.
	Comply with QHSE policies and procedures as well as comply with safe work methods as required.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by your Line Manger.


Person Specifications
Education:


	B.Sc
	MBA and MSc Degree is desirable


Professional Qualification:


	&nbsp;ISO 9001, 45001, 14001 and ISM Lead Auditor certifications, ISPS,
	STCW and MLC Implementation and Audit Courses
	ISM &amp; ISO Lead Auditor certificate


Experience:


	8-10 years of previous experience in marine offshore/maritime shipping, construction, oil and gas, etc with 5 years in leadership role.


Key Skills and&nbsp;competencies


	Quality Maritime Safety Management System Implementation.
	Marine Operations Risk Assessment and Management
	In-depth knowledge of Flag States, Port Authorities, Classification Society and International Maritime Regulatory bodies.
	Attention to details and Confidentiality
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Nestoil"
    
  },

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

}



Compliance and Regulatory Coordinator Job at Nestoil




        
    

        
        Nestoil is Nigeria’s largest indigenous Engineering, Procurement, Construction and Commissioning (EPCC) Company in the Oil and Gas sector and has been a significant contributor to local content in the industry since inception about 30 years ago. With about 2,000 direct employees, Nestoil continues to redefine industry standards in Pipeline Construction, Repairs and Maintenance with associated facilities for Dredging, River Crossing and Shoreline Protection.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Compliance and Regulatory Coordinator

Job Location: Warri, Delta
SBU: White Dove Shipping Company Limited

Job Summary & Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Accountable for managing all statutory/regulatory compliance framework applicable to White Dove Shipping Company activities such as international classification societies, flag states and all Nigerian authorities – NPA, NIMASA, DPR, NIWA, etc.
	These include all ISO compliance audits, local authorities’ inspections, ISM/vessel inspections and acquisitions of certificates, permits, licenses, consents, etc.


Responsibilities


	Validate the quality, safety and security systems of the Company and the vessel fleet at all times; ascertain its compliance with the industry and the Company standards.
	Pay regular visit to vessels for physical inspections to ensure all statutory compliance are in place.
	Conduct on board training, pre-vetting inspection and participate in the vetting inspections onboard the company managed vessels.
	Review and modify Procedures and Instructions where necessary to improve Safety Management System.
	Ensure all regulatory requirements in terms of documentations, certifications, authorization, procedures and regulations are complied with strictly.
	Process all DPR / Regulatory Barging permits.
	Plan, monitor and co-ordinate the vessels’ survey & audit schedule.
	Maintain corrective action register for all Marine related audits and inspections as well following up on same.
	Monitor Flag State Control (NIMASA) on vessels Survey and Audit result.
	Manage and keep updated the list of publications required onboard the vessels and advise on a Company standard to follow.
	Implement and monitor Company ISM Procedures.
	Conduct Independent compliance reviews on Marine Terminal Management & Self-Assessment (MTMSA) and Tanker Management & Self-Assessment (TMSA) Audits, prepare and submit comprehensive report on findings;
	Follow up with vessels on review findings and effective close out of observations
	Represent the Company at OCIMF (Oil Company International Marine Forum) meetings.
	Manage the Company’s SIRE (Ship Inspection Report Program) inspection process, conduct quality reviews of reports submitted and update OCIMF SIRE database.
	Comply with QHSE policies and procedures as well as comply with safe work methods as required.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned by your Line Manger.


Person Specifications
Education:


	B.Sc
	MBA and MSc Degree is desirable


Professional Qualification:


	 ISO 9001, 45001, 14001 and ISM Lead Auditor certifications, ISPS,
	STCW and MLC Implementation and Audit Courses
	ISM & ISO Lead Auditor certificate


Experience:


	8-10 years of previous experience in marine offshore/maritime shipping, construction, oil and gas, etc with 5 years in leadership role.


Key Skills and competencies


	Quality Maritime Safety Management System Implementation.
	Marine Operations Risk Assessment and Management
	In-depth knowledge of Flag States, Port Authorities, Classification Society and International Maritime Regulatory bodies.
	Attention to details and Confidentiality
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Supervisor",
  "description": "ValueMart is a fast-growing neighborhood supermarket committed to providing families and individuals with a convenient, reliable, and affordable shopping experience. Located in Lagos, ValueMart offers a well-curated selection of essential goods ranging from groceries, beverages, household items, toiletries, cosmetics, baby products, fresh produce, snacks, and more.

Our focus is quality, fair pricing, customer comfort, and consistency.&nbsp;We aim to create an environment where customers can walk in, find what they want easily, and enjoy outstanding service from our trained staff. At ValueMart, we believe that shopping should be stress-free, quick, and enjoyable, making us the preferred choice for residents and businesses in our area.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Abule-Egba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	Oversee daily operations and ensure all sections run smoothly.
	Supervise staff, assign duties, and ensure compliance with company policies.
	Monitor customer service standards and resolve customer complaints.
	Ensure cleanliness, orderliness, and proper product display.
	Track stock levels and report shortages or discrepancies.
	Support cashier oversight, shift handover, and cash control checks.
	Ensure staff punctuality, discipline, and performance.
	Report daily activities and issues to management.
	Drive sales, reduce waste, and maintain operational efficiency.


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any field.
	2&ndash;5 years of proven experience as a Supervisor in a supermarket, retail, fast-food, or hospitality environment.
	Strong leadership, communication, and people-management skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Good knowledge of customer service standards.
	Integrity, discipline, and strong problem-solving abilities.
	Must be able to work mornings, evenings, weekends, and holidays.
	Must live within or close to Abule-Egba / Agege / Iyana-Ipaja / Alimosho / Alakuko.


Salary
N110,000 - N140,000 / month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Updated CV, Application letter (stating position applied for) and Valid contact details&nbsp; to:&nbsp;otunbawilliams.a@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Application for [Position Name]&quot; as the subject of the mail. (Eg - Application for Store Supervisor).",

  },

Supervisor Job at ValueMart




        
    

        
        ValueMart is a fast-growing neighborhood supermarket committed to providing families and individuals with a convenient, reliable, and affordable shopping experience. Located in Lagos, ValueMart offers a well-curated selection of essential goods ranging from groceries, beverages, household items, toiletries, cosmetics, baby products, fresh produce, snacks, and more.

Our focus is quality, fair pricing, customer comfort, and consistency. We aim to create an environment where customers can walk in, find what they want easily, and enjoy outstanding service from our trained staff. At ValueMart, we believe that shopping should be stress-free, quick, and enjoyable, making us the preferred choice for residents and businesses in our area.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supervisor

Job Location: Abule-Egba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	Oversee daily operations and ensure all sections run smoothly.
	Supervise staff, assign duties, and ensure compliance with company policies.
	Monitor customer service standards and resolve customer complaints.
	Ensure cleanliness, orderliness, and proper product display.
	Track stock levels and report shortages or discrepancies.
	Support cashier oversight, shift handover, and cash control checks.
	Ensure staff punctuality, discipline, and performance.
	Report daily activities and issues to management.
	Drive sales, reduce waste, and maintain operational efficiency.


Requirements


	OND / HND / B.Sc. in any field.
	2–5 years of proven experience as a Supervisor in a supermarket, retail, fast-food, or hospitality environment.
	Strong leadership, communication, and people-management skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Good knowledge of customer service standards.
	Integrity, discipline, and strong problem-solving abilities.
	Must be able to work mornings, evenings, weekends, and holidays.
	Must live within or close to Abule-Egba / Agege / Iyana-Ipaja / Alimosho / Alakuko.


Salary
N110,000 - N140,000 / month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Updated CV, Application letter (stating position applied for) and Valid contact details  to: [email protected] using "Application for [Position Name]" as the subject of the mail. (Eg - Application for Store Supervisor).    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Albacorre Consulting is a dynamic consulting firm dedicated to bridging talent with opportunity. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, high-quality solutions for clients and candidates alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Chevron, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly competent, polished, and proactive Executive Personal Assistant to provide direct support to the Creative Director.
	This role requires a high level of professionalism, discretion, organization, and the ability to manage both business and personal administrative tasks in a fast-paced interior design and project management environment.
	The ideal candidate must be smart, well-presented, confident, and able to take initiative without constant supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide executive and personal administrative support to the Creative Director
	Manage daily calendar, meetings, site visits, and appointments
	Coordinate communication with clients, contractors, vendors, and consultants
	Follow up on ongoing projects to ensure timelines are met
	Prepare reports, presentations, and official documents
	Manage emails, calls, and correspondence professionally
	Assist with project scheduling and task tracking
	Arrange travel, meetings, and event schedules when required
	Maintain strict confidentiality on all business and personal matters
	Run official and personal errands when necessary
	Support basic business coordination including social media, content, and brand activities when required
	Ensure the Creative Director&rsquo;s workflow is smooth and well organized at all times


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Management, or related field
	3&ndash;5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant / Personal Assistant
	Excellent spoken and written English
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	High level of professionalism and emotional intelligence
	Proficient in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and scheduling tools
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Must be smart, confident, and well presented
	Experience in interior design, construction, architecture, or real estate is a strong advantage


Skills &amp; Personal Qualities:


	Discretion and confidentiality
	Strong attention to detail
	Ability to take initiative
	Problem-solving mindset
	Time management
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;praise.isaiah@albacorrre.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-04",

Executive Personal Assistant Job at Albacorre Consulting




        
    

        
        Albacorre Consulting is a dynamic consulting firm dedicated to bridging talent with opportunity. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, high-quality solutions for clients and candidates alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Personal Assistant

Job Location: Chevron, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly competent, polished, and proactive Executive Personal Assistant to provide direct support to the Creative Director.
	This role requires a high level of professionalism, discretion, organization, and the ability to manage both business and personal administrative tasks in a fast-paced interior design and project management environment.
	The ideal candidate must be smart, well-presented, confident, and able to take initiative without constant supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide executive and personal administrative support to the Creative Director
	Manage daily calendar, meetings, site visits, and appointments
	Coordinate communication with clients, contractors, vendors, and consultants
	Follow up on ongoing projects to ensure timelines are met
	Prepare reports, presentations, and official documents
	Manage emails, calls, and correspondence professionally
	Assist with project scheduling and task tracking
	Arrange travel, meetings, and event schedules when required
	Maintain strict confidentiality on all business and personal matters
	Run official and personal errands when necessary
	Support basic business coordination including social media, content, and brand activities when required
	Ensure the Creative Director’s workflow is smooth and well organized at all times


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Management, or related field
	3–5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant / Personal Assistant
	Excellent spoken and written English
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	High level of professionalism and emotional intelligence
	Proficient in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and scheduling tools
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Must be smart, confident, and well presented
	Experience in interior design, construction, architecture, or real estate is a strong advantage


Skills & Personal Qualities:


	Discretion and confidentiality
	Strong attention to detail
	Ability to take initiative
	Problem-solving mindset
	Time management
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Support Specialist",
  "description": "Ambiance Cafeteria has been in business for years and has grown into a premier name in the hospitality industry, offering a seamless blend of comfort, entertainment, and culinary excellence. what began as a single hospitality venture has evolved into a dynamic enterprise comprising a luxury hotel, a vibrant nightclub, an upscale restaurant, and a selection of stylishbars. With a commitment to outstanding guest experiences, Ambiance Cafeteria has consistently set new standards in service, ambiance, and innovation. Each facet of the business reflects a dedication to quality, from the refined accomodations of our hotel to the immersive atmosphere of our nightlife venues and the exceptional cuisine served in our restaurant. Over the years,&nbsp; Ambiance Cafeteria&nbsp;has become a preferred destination for both local and international guests, earning a reputation for excellence and reliability in the hospitality sector. Our continued growth is driven by passion for hospitality and a vision to create memorable experiences for every guest.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support&nbsp;Specialist

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented IT Support Specialist with 2&ndash;4 years of hands-on experience, particularly within a hospitality environment.
	The ideal candidate will have strong problem-solving abilities, a customer-focused mindset, and experience supporting IT systems in hotels, or similar service-driven settings.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide first- and second-line technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues
	Maintain and troubleshoot Property Management Systems (PMS), POS systems, and guest-facing technologies
	Support IT infrastructure including servers, workstations, printers, and network devices
	Ensure minimal downtime of IT systems critical to hotel operations
	Assist in system upgrades, installations, and configurations
	Monitor system performance and implement preventive maintenance measures
	Respond promptly to service requests and incidents, ensuring high levels of user satisfaction
	Collaborate with vendors and third-party service providers when required
	Maintain accurate documentation of issues, resolutions, and system configurations


Requirements


	OND in Information Technology, Computer Science or other related discipline.
	2&ndash;4 years of IT support experience, preferably in the hospitality industry
	Strong knowledge of Windows OS, networking fundamentals, and common IT systems
	Familiarity with hospitality software (e.g., PMS, POS systems) is highly desirable
	Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays if required


Preferred Skills:


	Experience with cloud-based systems and virtualization
	IT certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft certifications) are an advantage.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits package
	Opportunity to work in a dynamic hospitality environment
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV and Application letter &nbsp;to:&nbsp; ambiancehr18@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;IT SUPPORT FOR IMMEDIATE START&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-18",

IT Support Specialist Job at Ambiance Cafeteria




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Ambiance Cafeteria has been in business for years and has grown into a premier name in the hospitality industry, offering a seamless blend of comfort, entertainment, and culinary excellence. what began as a single hospitality venture has evolved into a dynamic enterprise comprising a luxury hotel, a vibrant nightclub, an upscale restaurant, and a selection of stylishbars. With a commitment to outstanding guest experiences, Ambiance Cafeteria has consistently set new standards in service, ambiance, and innovation. Each facet of the business reflects a dedication to quality, from the refined accomodations of our hotel to the immersive atmosphere of our nightlife venues and the exceptional cuisine served in our restaurant. Over the years,  Ambiance Cafeteria has become a preferred destination for both local and international guests, earning a reputation for excellence and reliability in the hospitality sector. Our continued growth is driven by passion for hospitality and a vision to create memorable experiences for every guest.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Specialist

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented IT Support Specialist with 2–4 years of hands-on experience, particularly within a hospitality environment.
	The ideal candidate will have strong problem-solving abilities, a customer-focused mindset, and experience supporting IT systems in hotels, or similar service-driven settings.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide first- and second-line technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues
	Maintain and troubleshoot Property Management Systems (PMS), POS systems, and guest-facing technologies
	Support IT infrastructure including servers, workstations, printers, and network devices
	Ensure minimal downtime of IT systems critical to hotel operations
	Assist in system upgrades, installations, and configurations
	Monitor system performance and implement preventive maintenance measures
	Respond promptly to service requests and incidents, ensuring high levels of user satisfaction
	Collaborate with vendors and third-party service providers when required
	Maintain accurate documentation of issues, resolutions, and system configurations


Requirements


	OND in Information Technology, Computer Science or other related discipline.
	2–4 years of IT support experience, preferably in the hospitality industry
	Strong knowledge of Windows OS, networking fundamentals, and common IT systems
	Familiarity with hospitality software (e.g., PMS, POS systems) is highly desirable
	Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays if required


Preferred Skills:


	Experience with cloud-based systems and virtualization
	IT certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft certifications) are an advantage.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits package
	Opportunity to work in a dynamic hospitality environment
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Application letter  to:  [email protected] using "IT SUPPORT FOR IMMEDIATE START" as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply
